steps.title: Write obtuse in terms of acute
steps.content:
A transversal across parallel lines creates four equal acute angles and four equal obtuse angles. Each obtuse angle is supplementary to an acute angle:
$$\text{obtuse}=180^{\circ}-\text{acute}$$

steps.title: Build the requested sum
steps.content:
The sum of one acute angle and three obtuse angles is
$$\text{acute}+3(180^{\circ}-\text{acute})$$
$$=540^{\circ}-2\text{acute}$$
Substitute the acute angle measure $(8x-690)^{\circ}$:
$$540-2(8x-690)=540-16x+1{,}380$$
$$=-16x+1{,}920$$

steps.title: Match the constant term
steps.content: The problem gives the same sum in the form $(-16x+w)^{\circ}$. Therefore the constant term is $w$: $$w=1920$$ The answer is 1920.
